spec_match.rationale: Settings UI exercises AccentColor on header, active-tab indicator, toggle track, focus-visible outline, primary+secondary buttons, plus a live AccentColorText readability table — covers the full surface the spec defines.

interactive.rationale: Six preset swatches plus a custom color picker, all driving real-time updates of the panel + a three-row WCAG contrast table; toggles are live (checkbox-controlled).

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a settings panel themed entirely from AccentColor with a live contrast checker confirming WCAG AA. Delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Contrast checker is good and the page now probes keyword support plus standalone display-mode gating, but forced-colors fallback is still missing and the 'AccentColor on bg-ivory' failures are reported without author guidance.

references.rationale: see-also links the chromestatus feature URL directly.

design_system.rationale: All neutrals come from --bg-* / --text-* / --border-black variables; the page uses CSS color-mix for soft-badge tints rather than raw hex; primary-btn overrides both background and color together.

openQuestions.title: Make contrast failures actionable
openQuestions.detail: When the WCAG checker reports FAIL on AccentColor-on-white or AccentColor-on-bg-ivory, the demo should suggest a fix (e.g. 'wrap your text in a tinted container' or 'use color-mix(in srgb, AccentColor 90%, black) for body text on light backgrounds'). Right now it shows the failure but leaves the author without guidance.

openQuestions.title: Add a swatch for the duplicated CSS-variable presets
openQuestions.detail: Three of the six swatches use background:var(--accent-blue) but pass data-accent values that differ from --accent-blue, so the visible swatch color does not match the actual accent the click applies. Pick distinct background hexes or wire each swatch to its own data-accent to avoid confusion.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate forced-colors interaction
openQuestions.detail: Add a 'simulate forced-colors' toggle showing the @media (forced-colors: active) variant of the panel — that's where AccentColor really lights up because it ties to OS-set high-contrast palettes.

summary: A real-feeling settings panel with live contrast verification, proper design-system hygiene, and an explicit installed-PWA runtime probe. Remaining issues are unrelated to the PWA gate: contrast failures need author guidance, preset swatches do not all match their applied accents, and forced-colors interaction is still not demonstrated.
